title: Lead Management
description: Master the lead entry point, lead stages, qualification framework, and assignment rules.

sections.title: Where Everything Starts
sections.content:
The Lead is the entry point for all new prospects entering NxN's commercial system. This is foundational: every new prospect—without exception—starts as a Lead.

Why? Because a Lead is lightweight and flexible. You can create it from anywhere (marketing systems, prospecting lists, partner referrals, inbound requests). If it turns out to be invalid, a duplicate, or not a fit, you can research, merge, or disqualify it without cluttering the account database. If it's valid and qualified, it converts into the proper account hierarchy and becomes an Opportunity.

Leads are your quality control layer.

sections.title: Where Leads Come From
sections.content:
Leads originate from various sources:

• Inbound inquiries — Website forms, demos, content requests
• Partner referrals — Trusted partners introducing qualified prospects
• Prospecting lists — Database lists, LinkedIn, industry lists
• Marketing campaigns — Email, webinars, events
• Sales prospecting — Direct outreach, cold calls
• Customer referrals — Existing customers recommending others

Some sources are "Trusted" (pre-qualified, verified, from established partners). Others are "Untrusted" (require thorough verification). A trusted partner referral might need light verification. A generic prospecting list needs detailed company and contact verification.

Trust but verify.

sections.title: How Leads Progress
sections.content:
Leads move through defined stages as you take action:

1. New — Created, not yet contacted
2. Contacted — You've made first contact
3. Verified — Company, contact, and need are confirmed as real and valid
4. Qualified — Meets all five qualification criteria (see next section)
5. Unqualified — Does not meet criteria; rejected
6. Nurture — Shows interest, but timing not right yet
7. Converted — Has become an Account and Opportunity

Movement isn't automatic. You drive it forward through phone calls, emails, meetings, and research. Document each stage change in the lead record.

sections.title: The Five Qualification Criteria
sections.content:
A lead is qualified when it meets all five criteria:

1. Company Validity — The company exists, operates in the stated industry/geography

2. Contact Validity — The contact person exists, has legitimate contact info, has authority or influence

3. Defined Need — They've expressed a specific business need aligned with your offering

4. Budget or Funding — They've confirmed (explicitly or by implication) they have budget available

5. Timeframe — They've indicated a decision timeline (e.g., "implement by Q3")

All five required. If you're missing one, decide: Nurture (interest but timing unclear) or Unqualified (unlikely to progress).

Qualification discipline saves time and improves conversion rates.

sections.title: Assignment & Enrichment
sections.content:
Assignment: When a lead enters the system, assignment rules automatically route it to the right sales rep based on geography, account assignment, industry vertical, or other criteria. A banking prospect in New York goes to the banking specialist in that region. This ensures the best person pursues each lead.

Enrichment: As leads progress, enrich them with context:

• Company industry and size
• Contact job title and decision authority
• Technology stack and competitors
• Company growth, funding, recent news

Enrichment through research, third-party data, or LinkedIn enables faster, more effective qualification and conversion.

sections.title: Duplicates & Scoring
sections.content:
Duplicate Management: Leads entering the system can be duplicates of existing leads or contacts. The system flags potential duplicates using matching rules. Identify and merge duplicates so you don't waste effort pursuing the same prospect twice.

Lead Scoring (optional): Some organizations score leads based on engagement and fit. Downloaded resources? Visited pricing page? Higher engagement score. High-value industry or company size? Higher profile score. Scoring helps you prioritize which leads to pursue most actively.

The quality of your leads determines the quality of your opportunities. Invest in proper lead creation, verification, and qualification. Your entire sales process becomes more efficient and win rates improve.

keyTakeaways: Leads must start in the system; every prospect must be verified and qualified using a five-criteria framework
Assignment rules route leads to the right sales representative based on geography, vertical, or other criteria
Duplicate matching at lead entry prevents costly duplicate pursuit and keeps the database clean
